articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Estêvão, Rodrygo star as Brazil hammer South Korea
article.description: Estêvão and Rodrygo both scored braces as Brazil cruised to a 5-0 friendly win over South Korea.
article.linkText: Estêvão, Rodrygo star as Brazil hammer Korea

images.name: Do Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil?
images.caption: The ESPN FC crew debate if Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil than Real Madrid.

video.headline: Do Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil?
video.caption: The ESPN FC crew debate if Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil than Real Madrid.
video.title: Do Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil?
video.description: The ESPN FC crew debate if Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil than Real Madrid.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Analysis (Do Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil? ) GLOBAL 2025-10-11

article.story:
Estêvão and Rodrygo both scored braces as Brazil cruised to a 5-0 friendly win over South Korea.

Chelsea forward Estêvão, 18, opened the scoring for the visitors at the Seoul World Cup Stadium after slotting home from Newcastle captain Bruno Guimaraes' pass in the 13th minute.

Rodrygo then doubled their advantage in the 41st minute, latching onto a pass from skipper Casemiro before rolling into the bottom corner.

- Source: No injuries reported after Vini Jr. house fire
- The race to the 2026 World Cup: Who has qualified?

The duo continued to shine and scored within three minutes of each other immediately after the restart as Estêvão struck again in the 47th minute before Rodrygo bagged his second only two minutes later.

Madrid's Vinícius Júnior then capped off a dominant display, breaking down the pitch and firing home in the 77th minute.

"Our journey to the World Cup started today and started well because the team played very well. I enjoyed the game, obviously, because the team showed great quality," Brazil head coach Carlo Ancelotti said.

Brazil are set to play one more friendly this international window when they face Japan on Tuesday.

"Today the team showed it can play at this level, with intensity, quality, and commitment. Since I've been here, I've been talking about collective commitment. This is a foundation, and when the team has this foundation, we have a great chance of doing things very well," Ancelotti added.

videos.headline: Do Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil?
videos.description: The ESPN FC crew debate if Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil than Real Madrid.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Analysis (Do Vinicius Jr & Rodrygo play with more freedom for Brazil? ) GLOBAL 2025-10-11
